items.rationale: His core thesis: blockchains and tokens are the only viable way to restore the internet as an open, user-owned platform that rewards creators rather than Big Tech aggregators. This animates his entire book and public work.

citations.quote: I explain why crypto/web3 is the only plausible path to sustaining the original vision of the internet as an open platform
we needed a book that explains the full potential of blockchains and tokens, and how these technologies can support productive and not just speculative use cases

items.rationale: His most concrete recent capital deployment. Excited about player-owned assets, composability, interoperability, and permanence — open economies and modding moved to the foreground. Backed CCP/EVE ($40M) and Proof of Play.

citations.quote: I'm thrilled to share that @a16z is leading a $40M investment in @CCPGames' new AAA gaming project set in the EVE Universe
we're leading a seed investment in @ProofOfPlay... committed to building a future with composability, interoperability, and permanence at the core of gameplay

items.rationale: Continues to advocate a personal 'vision for NFTs' tied to players and creators owning what they make online; underpins his gaming and creator-economy theses.
items.age_note: Most recent explicit reference 2024-03-15; consistent with earlier on-chain-asset enthusiasm.

citations.quote: Thanks @msuster for hosting me at Upfront Summit to talk about Read Write Own, how we invest for the long term, my vision for NFTs, and more

items.rationale: Personally active daily on Farcaster and prefers its community to Twitter's — a real-usage signal of his bet on user-owned social platforms over incumbent web2 networks.

citations.quote: I use Farcaster all the time. The community is far better than on Twitter.

items.rationale: Treats AI as a major intersecting technology he writes about and invests around (e.g., decentralized compute via Gensyn). Genuinely engaged though it sits beside, not at the center of, his crypto focus.
items.age_note: Enthusiasm steady; 2024-01-18 remark notes AI arrived slower than he expected, a mild timing caveat not a reversal.

citations.quote: today we announced our newest investment in UK-based @gensynai
Sure, timelines in tech are hard to predict. I also thought AI would come sooner.

items.rationale: Sees VR following the cost-down curve of prior computing platforms once Meta and Apple invest. A personal enthusiasm but peripheral to his crypto mandate.
items.age_note: Stated once on 2023-06-05 (~10 months old) and never restated; treat as persisted, not current focus.

citations.quote: Excited for Vision Pro. With Meta and now Apple investment, VR will follow the same pattern as past computing platforms, quickly becoming cheaper, faster, lighter

items.rationale: His most consistent worry: gambling/speculation culture damages public perception and undermines crypto's productive potential. He explicitly devotes a book section to it and wants it stamped out.

citations.quote: stamping out the harmful "casino" culture that has developed around crypto
the harmful casino culture that has developed around blockchains that hurts their public perception and undermines their potential

items.rationale: Believes the absence of clear market-structure regulation imposes heavy compliance costs, risks pushing well-intentioned startups offshore, and lets bad actors flourish. Drives his policy and PAC activism.

citations.quote: hundreds of crypto/web3 companies in the US who are similarly trying to follow the law, often with little guidance on how to comply
certain policymakers believe it should be banned, while other people think it should have no guardrails. Neither of those options will allow the technology to reach its full potential

items.rationale: Views unscrupulous, law-skirting competitors as having created an uneven playing field that harmed compliant builders and consumers; wants enforcement to remove them.

citations.quote: Competitors like FTX and Binance were taking market share and rapidly introducing new products in ways that... exceeded the boundaries of the law

items.rationale: The incumbent threat his whole thesis opposes: value and control concentrated in Big Tech aggregators rather than the users and creators who make the internet valuable.

citations.quote: realign the future of the Internet away from Big Tech to the people who use it

risk_regime.rationale: Dixon is a structurally bullish long-term builder, not a tactical trader. As of early 2024 — post-FTX, launching his book — he frames crypto/web3 as 'early innings' and keeps deploying capital into gaming, AI infra, and international expansion. His risk-on posture is explicitly conditional: he wants speculation curbed and clear regulation in place, so it is conviction in building the technology, not in token price speculation.

notes: Corpus is heavily dominated by book-promotion (Read Write Own, Jan 2024) and policy/regulatory advocacy; relatively few statements name specific assets. No individual tokens or tickers are mentioned, so token lists are empty. Strongest and most current convictions cluster around the open-internet thesis, web3 gaming, and anti-casino-culture/pro-regulation positions — all reinforced repeatedly through Q1 2024. VR/Vision Pro is the one notable view that is old (June 2023) and unrepeated, marked persisted. No material reversals detected; the AI 'came slower than expected' remark is a timing caveat, not a stance change. Horizons skew structural throughout — Dixon rarely expresses tactical/market-timing views.
